brief_summary: Three-lane timeline chart for the H2 'Restock cadence — the quiet advantage'. Top lane is HMX at ~8 weeks (bronze-emphasized with 7 evenly-spaced restock ticks across a 52-week span, labelled 'sweet spot'). Middle lane is Gateron at quarterly cadence (4 sparser ticks). Bottom lane is Cherry as a continuous dashed line with a faint background wash, labelled 'always on, never news'. The visual argument matches the article body: 8 weeks is tight enough that vendors carry it as default but loose enough that each restock is news.
data_sources: hmx-cloud-deep-dive.mdx § 'Restock cadence — the quiet advantage' — 'HMX restocks at the major North American vendors on roughly an eight-week cycle ... Gateron's cadence on the Oil King and Pro 3.0 line runs closer to quarterly ... Cherry's cadence is functionally infinite — the MX2A is a production-line product and is always available.'
